items.prompt: In a rotated ascending array with distinct values, which condition shows that the left half [left, mid] is ordered?

feedback.reason: With distinct values, if the left endpoint is no greater than mid, no rotation pivot lies inside [left, mid].
feedback.details: That ordered half can then be tested for target membership.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.mid_le_right: That identifies the right half as ordered.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.target_le_mid: Target comparison does not determine whether the half itself is ordered.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.left_le_right: This may show the whole interval is ordered, but it is not the standard left-half test in the rotated branch.

items.prompt: If nums[left] > nums[mid] in a rotated array with distinct values, what can be concluded?

options.text: The right half [mid, right] is ordered.

feedback.reason: With one rotation and distinct values, the pivot must lie in the left portion when nums[left] > nums[mid].
feedback.details: The segment from mid through right therefore remains ascending.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.left_ordered: The pivot lies inside the left side.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.target_absent: The comparison reveals structure, not target existence.

items.prompt: The left half is ordered and satisfies nums[left] <= target < nums[mid]. Which side should be retained after nums[mid] is known not to equal target?

options.text: The left side ending at mid - 1.
The right side starting at mid + 1.

feedback.reason: The ordered left half contains every value between its endpoint values exactly once under the distinct-value contract.
feedback.details: The target's range membership proves the right half cannot contain the desired occurrence.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.right_side: The target lies inside the value range of the ordered left half.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.both_sides: The ordered-range test provides a legal discard rule.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.return_mid: Mid is known not to match.

items.prompt: The left half is ordered, but target is smaller than nums[left] or greater than nums[mid]. Which side should be retained?

options.text: The right side starting at mid + 1.

feedback.reason: Range exclusion from the ordered half permits discarding it.
feedback.details: The other half may contain the pivot and the target.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.left_side: The target falls outside that half's value range.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.return_left: No endpoint equality has been established.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.stop: The target may remain in the rotated right half.

items.prompt: How should the algorithm behave when the active interval is not rotated and is fully ascending?

options.text: The same ordered-half logic reduces to ordinary binary search.
Return not found because no pivot exists inside the interval.
Switch unconditionally to a full linear scan.

feedback.reason: An unrotated interval has an ordered left half and an ordered right half.
feedback.details: The target-range test chooses the same side as classic binary search.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.fail_rotation: Zero rotation is a valid rotated-array case.

items.prompt: A one-element rotated array contains target. What should happen?

options.text: The midpoint is index 0 and should be returned.
The loop should skip because no half can be identified.
Both boundaries should move before comparison.

feedback.reason: Rotated search retains the exact-match check before structural half classification.
feedback.details: Minimal domains should behave like classic exact search.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.skip_singleton: A singleton is a valid candidate interval.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.move_both: The only candidate must be checked first.

items.prompt: Why is comparing target only with nums[mid] insufficient after a mismatch in a rotated array?

options.text: The global order is broken at the pivot, so direction requires identifying an ordered half and checking its endpoint range.
nums[mid] cannot be read after rotation.
Targets in rotated arrays are never numeric.

feedback.reason: A simple target < nums[mid] comparison may point across the rotation pivot incorrectly.
feedback.details: Local half ordering restores a valid elimination rule.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.target_not_numeric: The reasoning applies to any ordered values.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.both_halves_unsorted: At least one half is ordered under the distinct-value contract.

items.prompt: What problem do equal values create when nums[left] == nums[mid] == nums[right]?

options.text: The comparisons may not reveal which half contains the rotation pivot.
The input can no longer be considered rotated.

feedback.reason: Distinct-value reasoning relies on a strict structural comparison.
feedback.details: When all three values are equal, shrinking one or both boundaries may be necessary, which can reduce the worst-case progress.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.target_found: The equal endpoint value may differ from target.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.array_invalid: Rotated arrays may contain duplicates under a broader contract.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.mid_overflow: Value equality is unrelated to index arithmetic.

items.prompt: When duplicate ambiguity is resolved by incrementing left and decrementing right, what worst-case behavior becomes possible?

feedback.reason: Repeated equal endpoints can force the algorithm to discard only one or two positions per iteration.
feedback.details: Correctness can be preserved while the logarithmic guarantee is lost.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.constant: Boundary shrinking may inspect many equal values.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.log_squared: The interval may shrink by one rather than by half.

items.prompt: An ascending array is rotated by one position so the former last element moves to index 0. Which edge case does this test?

options.text: The rotation pivot lies immediately after the first element.

feedback.reason: Rotations near either boundary test whether ordered-half detection works when one sorted region is very small.
feedback.details: The search must not assume the pivot lies near the middle.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.no_rotation: Moving the last element to the front changes the pivot.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.descending_order: Most of the array remains ascending.

items.prompt: The left half [left, mid] is ordered. Which conditions place target inside that half? Select all that apply.

options.text: target equals nums[mid], before the mismatch branch is entered.

feedback.reason: The ordered half covers its inclusive endpoint-value range under distinct values.
feedback.details: The exact-match check at mid is normally handled before half selection.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.greater_mid: That lies outside the ordered left-half range.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.strict_inside: The target lies strictly inside the ordered range.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.at_mid: Mid itself is part of the left half and should be returned.

items.prompt: Which test cases are important for rotated-array search? Select all that apply.

options.text: Pivot near the first or last position.
Only one case where target begins at the first midpoint.

feedback.reason: Rotated search needs structural edge cases, not just a midpoint equality case.
feedback.details: Absent targets should also be represented across these shapes.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.only_middle_hit: This does not exercise structural branch logic.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.no_rotation: The algorithm should reduce to classic search.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.pivot_edges: These stress highly unbalanced ordered halves.

items.prompt: Which statements are correct when duplicates are allowed? Select all that apply.

options.text: Equal endpoint and midpoint values can make the ordered half ambiguous.
A correct strategy may shrink equal boundaries before continuing.
Worst-case time may degrade to O(n).
The search is always O(log n) despite arbitrary duplicates.

feedback.reason: Duplicates weaken the structural information used for half elimination.
feedback.details: The algorithm can remain correct but may lose logarithmic worst-case progress.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.always_log: An all-equal interval disproves this guarantee.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.ordered_half_ambiguous: The pivot cannot be localized from equality.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.shrink_boundaries: This removes redundant ambiguity while preserving possible targets.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.worst_linear: Repeated one-step shrinking can be linear.

items.prompt: Arrange one distinct-value rotated-array search iteration.

elements.text: Determine whether the left or right half is ordered.
Check whether target lies inside the ordered half's endpoint range.
Retain that half if target is inside; otherwise retain the other half.

feedback.reason: Exact match is resolved first, then local order supports a range-based discard decision.
feedback.details: Every mismatch iteration removes mid and one impossible region.

items.prompt: Arrange the branch logic when the left half is ordered and mid is not target.

feedback.reason: The ordered-half endpoint range determines whether it can contain target.
feedback.details: The strict upper comparison excludes the already-checked midpoint.

items.prompt: Arrange a duplicate-ambiguity branch when nums[left] == nums[mid] == nums[right] and mid is not target.

elements.text: Recognize that neither half can be proven ordered from the equality.
Increment left and decrement right after confirming those endpoint values are not target.
Repeat structural analysis on the smaller interval.

feedback.reason: Equal nonmatching endpoints can be removed without losing target, but only limited progress is guaranteed.
feedback.details: This branch preserves correctness while explaining possible linear degradation.

items.prompt: A rotated sorted array contains n distinct values, and each mismatch discards about half of the active interval. Select worst-case time and iterative auxiliary space.

feedback.reason: Distinct values guarantee enough structural information to choose one side each iteration.
feedback.details: Only boundary and midpoint variables are required.

items.prompt: A rotated sorted array may contain arbitrary duplicates, and equal endpoints are removed one step at a time when structure is ambiguous. Select worst-case time and iterative auxiliary space.

feedback.reason: Duplicate ambiguity can prevent half elimination and force linear boundary shrinking.
feedback.details: The implementation still uses constant extra state.
